question.text: Under SEBI (Investment Advisers) Regulations, 2013, which of the following statements about the segregation of advisory and distribution activities is CORRECT?

answer.text: An individual investment adviser shall not provide distribution services
An individual investment adviser can provide both advisory and distribution services to the same client
Non-individual investment advisers are not required to maintain client-level segregation
Family members of an individual IA can freely provide distribution services to the same client

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Regulation 22 explicitly states that an individual investment adviser shall not provide distribution services. Furthermore, family members of the individual IA shall not provide distribution services to the same client. **The Trap:** Non-individual IAs must also maintain client-level segregation at the group level -- the same client cannot receive both advisory and distribution services. **Cross-Exam Context:** This segregation requirement is a key compliance topic in NISM Series X-A and Series III-A exams. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A client must choose to be either an advisory client or a distribution client at the group/family level -- they cannot be both.
